rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GOTTSCHALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 24, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is continued to June 6 per agreement of the parties. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CLINTON BOGAN JR. et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION (SUED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 24, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Rapid-American Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under the factually devoid prong and the negation of an element prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bogan was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Weiner¿s declaration are sustained as to paragraphs 4 and 6 and overruled as to paragraph 5. Plaintiff¿s objections to the purported Carey-Canadian Mines Ltd. sales records and authenticating affidavit are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Lehnert¿s deposition testimony are sustained. Even if Mr. Lehnert¿s deposition testimony were admissible, they do not create a triable issue whether the Bestwall joint compound to which Mr. Bogan was exposed in 1963 contained asbestos fiber attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CLINTON BOGAN JR. et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION (SUED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 24, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Goulds Pumps, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bogan was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Bogan¿s deposition testimony, defendant¿s responses to interrogatories and Mr. Bradshaw¿s deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
